compiled_state.title: worktree-isolation-guard: main session Agent dispatch with isolation:worktree -> blocked without auth marker
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Problem

The `worktree-isolation-guard.sh` PreToolUse hook categorically blocks any Agent dispatch with `isolation: "worktree"` unless an auth marker file (`/tmp/worktree-isolation-authorized-<uuid>`) exists with a live PID. This auth-file pattern works for orchestrator skills (sprint, fix-bug, debug-everything) which write the marker before dispatch, but the main session / direct user dispatches do not write auth markers — causing legitimate worktree isolation requests to be denied.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. In any session (not inside a sprint/fix-bug/debug-everything workflow), dispatch an Agent with `isolation: "worktree""
2. The hook blocks with: `Worktree isolation is disabled for sub-agents`

## Expected Behavior

Main session Agent dispatches with `isolation: "worktree"` should be allowed. The guard should distinguish between:
- Main session dispatches (should be allowed — the session IS the orchestrator)
- Nested sub-agent dispatches (should be blocked unless auth marker exists)

## Root Cause

`plugins/dso/hooks/worktree-isolation-guard.sh` lines 33-63: the guard checks for auth marker files but has no fallback for main-session dispatches. The hook cannot currently distinguish the main session from a sub-agent.

## Relevant Files

- `plugins/dso/hooks/worktree-isolation-guard.sh` (the guard)
- `plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/worktree-dispatch.md` (auth marker protocol)

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 2 (BASIC). Hook code bug — worktree-isolation-guard blocks main session. Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Fix applied: Added zero-marker heuristic to worktree-isolation-guard.sh and session-misc-functions.sh. When no live auth markers exist (no orchestrator running), the guard auto-creates a marker and allows the dispatch, treating it as a main-session context.
comments.author: Test
